_note: Ready-to-insert interactive_image block for the human respiratory system. Image: respiratory-system_illus.webp (keyed transparent + compressed from the founder's generated PNG, 1448x1086 portrait). Note: the body-silhouette pixels in this particular generation were darker/more uneven than the digestive-system image, so the keyed webp shows a faint, slightly textured grey silhouette when viewed on a white background -- but it blends in cleanly on the app's actual dark reading background (#121316), confirmed by browser screenshot. To ship: upload the webp to R2 via the books asset-upload route, put the returned URL in `src`, and add this block to the target biology page (Class 11 Breathing and Exchange of Gases). Hotspots authored + browser-verified against the image on 2026-07-15 (all 9 placements confirmed live, including the trachea/bronchi/bronchioles cluster with no pill-overlap).
caption: 📸 Tap each dot to explore — or hit 'Test yourself' to play Label Sprint

hotspots.label: Nasal cavity
hotspots.detail: Warms, moistens, and filters incoming air; lined with mucus and fine hairs.

hotspots.label: Pharynx
hotspots.detail: The shared passage for both air and food, connecting the nasal/oral cavities to the larynx.

hotspots.label: Larynx
hotspots.detail: The **voice box** — contains the vocal cords and guards the entrance to the trachea.

hotspots.label: Trachea
hotspots.detail: The windpipe — a cartilage-ringed tube carrying air to the bronchi.

hotspots.label: Bronchi
hotspots.detail: The two main branches of the trachea, one entering each lung.

hotspots.label: Bronchioles
hotspots.detail: The fine branching airways inside the lung that lead to the alveoli.

hotspots.label: Lungs
hotspots.detail: The paired organs of gas exchange, made of millions of alveoli.

hotspots.label: Alveoli
hotspots.detail: Tiny grape-like air sacs, the actual site of **gas exchange** with the blood.

hotspots.label: Diaphragm
hotspots.detail: The dome-shaped muscle below the lungs that drives breathing by contracting and relaxing.

generation_prompt: A clean anatomically accurate educational illustration of the human respiratory system from the nasal cavity to the lungs, standard biology-textbook layout within a faint body-torso silhouette, semi-realistic painterly style, with one lung cut open to show branching airways. Show clearly separated: nasal cavity, pharynx, larynx, trachea, bronchi, bronchioles, lungs, alveoli, diaphragm. Muted palette, flat, solid deep-charcoal background #121316, no text/labels/numbers/leader lines. Portrait 3:4, high resolution.
